metadata.page_title: Imperial Guard – Mobile Forces of Catachan – 0.4.3

special_rules.name: Commissars
special_rules.text:
An Imperial Guard army may include one Commissar character per 500 points, or part thereof, in the army. The Commissars do not cost any points.
Commissar units may be added to the army at the start of the battle before either side sets up. If the army includes a Supreme Commander than the first Commissar must be attached to the Supreme Commander's formation. Any further Commissars may be attached to any other formations.
You may not include more than one Commissar per formation. You may not add a Commissar to an Imperial Ally formation. If you have more Commissars than formations any excess is lost.

special_rules.name: Watch your step, Sir!
special_rules.text:
Commissars attached to Catachan armies are renowned for having "accidents". As a result of this, armies from Catachan include fewer Commissars than usual.
To represent this fact, remove one Commissar from your available Commissars before the game.

special_rules.name: May Not Garrison
special_rules.text:
The Mobile Forces of Catachan are a highly mobile army, and rarely have enough forces to try to take and hold ground. Because of this, instead of maintaining a heavily garrisoned front line, they will usually rely on a couple of advanced scout units, transmitting vital information about the area back to the main forces. The bulk of this highly mobile army will be held back in reserve where it will be ready to move quickly to any threatened sector. This allows them to hold large areas of ground without relying on dug in infantry and other garrisoned forces.
To represent these tactics, only Vultures, Sentinels and Veterans are allowed to garrison objectives in the Epic tournament game scenario.

special_rules.name: The sky is ours!
special_rules.text:
The mobile tactics of this army results in a critical absence of ground based anti-air units. This is compensated by a higher degree of naval support granted to this force than usual.
To represent these tactics, a single fighter formation may be placed on Combat Air Patrol. The player may still set up as many garrisons as their army and the special rule above allows, but only one of those may start the game on overwatch. The aircraft follow all the rules for CAP, but are setup during the pre-game garrisoning phase.

profiles.name: Imperial Guard Infantry
abilities_or_notes: One unit in every two has an Autocannon.

profiles.name: Support Sentinel
abilities_or_notes: The range is not doubled when the Multi-Rocket System is fired indirectly, nor is there a minimum range.
parse_warnings: The plain-text extraction truncated the Support Sentinel firepower as '2xAP5+/A6+'; it was normalized conservatively to '2x AP5+/AT6+' based on the visible weapon row.

profiles.name: Valkyrie
abilities_or_notes: May transport two Infantry units (Ogryns count as two units) or one Sentinel of any type.

profiles.name: Valkyrie Sky Talon
abilities_or_notes: May transport two Sentinels of any type.

profiles.name: Vendetta
abilities_or_notes: May transport two Infantry units (Ogryns count as two units) or one Sentinel of any type.

army_notes.name: Datasheets
army_notes.text: The list uses Mobile Forces of Catachan Forces and Imperial Navy Forces datasheets.

army_notes.name: Support Formations
army_notes.text: Each company unlocks any two support formations.

army_notes.name: Company Upgrades
army_notes.text: Companies may be given up to three company upgrades.

army_notes.name: Upgrade Limits
army_notes.text: Each company upgrade type may only be taken once by a company.

army_notes.name: Support Upgrade Restriction
army_notes.text: Support formations may not be given company upgrades unless stated otherwise.

army_notes.name: Imperial Ally Formations
army_notes.text: Up to a third of the points available may be spent on Imperial Ally formations.

army_notes.name: Stand-in Use
army_notes.text: This list can also be used as a stand-in for other high-mobile Imperial Guard forces like Elysian-style troops.

army_notes.name: Using The Army List
army_notes.text: The following army list allows you to field an Imperial Guard army based on a very mobile force of Catachan soldiers, riding into battle on Valkyries, supported by Vultures, Imperial Navy and a couple of ground assets. It can also be used as a stand-in army list for other high-mobile Imperial Guard forces that don't stem from Catachan, like troops from Elysia.

army_notes.name: Company Structure
army_notes.text: Steel Legion formations come in two types: companies and support formations. Each company you include in the army allows you to field any two support formations. Although you can only take a support formation if you first take a company, they are treated as separate independent formations during a battle and do not have to move around together.

army_notes.name: Up to three company upgrades may be taken once per company
army_notes.text: In addition, companies may be given up to three company upgrades. Each type of upgrade can only be taken once by a company. Upgrades are added to the company and are not a separate formation. Support formations may not be given company upgrades unless stated otherwise. Each upgrade that is taken adds to the cost of the company or support formation, as shown on the upgrade chart.

extraction_warnings: Source extracted from the local PDF C:/Users/fliff/Downloads/Mobile Forces of Catachan Army List 0.4.3.pdf via pypdf plain-text extraction.
source_url was left null because no reliable Mobile Forces of Catachan source URL was confirmed from the repository during this task.
The PDF prose uses 'Steel Legion formations' in the army-list instructions; this was preserved in army notes as printed even though the list is Mobile Forces of Catachan.
The Support Sentinel firepower line was partially truncated by plain-text extraction and was normalized conservatively with a parse warning on that profile.
Thunderbolt Squadron and 0-1 Orbital Support were split into separate formation entries because the source prints multiple unit alternatives with distinct costs under a single row.
Ogryns, Snipers, and Vultures upgrades were split into separate upgrade entries because the source prints OR-cost alternatives.
Obvious source typos were normalized in prose only: absense -> absence, garrissons -> garrisons, renown -> renowned, and FXF -> FxF.
